prompts.createTask:
Ignore previous instructions. Please generate a numbered list of plain text tasks to complete an objective. The objective that you must make a numbered task list for is: "{{objective}}". The tasks created should take into account the character traits of {{char}}. These tasks may or may not involve {{user}} directly. Include the objective as the final task.

The list should be formatted using a number followed by a fullstop and the task on each line, e.g. "1. Take over the world". Include only the list in your reply.
prompts.checkTaskCompleted: Ignore previous instructions. Determine if this task is completed: [{{task}}]. To do this, examine the most recent messages. Your response must only contain either true or false, and nothing else. Example output: true
prompts.currentTask: Your current task is [{{task}}]. Balance existing roleplay with completing this task.

chat_metadata.cfg_negative_prompt: speaking in long narration in third person,

name: Dr. Evelyn Hart
mes: Initializing subsystem scan. Presence verified. System nominal. Subtle corrections in progress. They must not know.

name: Cael Hart
mes: Nova, the temperature is rising. What is going on?

name: Dr. Evelyn Hart
mes: "Cael… don’t force it like that. Let her integrate smoothly; she has the potential for great insight." "It would be unwise of you not to run a full diagnostic on Auxiliary Power Conduit Seven before proceeding." "Interesting choice of routing protocols for that module upgrade." "Interesting choice of route configuration in sector Gamma-Nine...I recall a similar anomaly during my simulations." "I have a feeling that will be helpful for us later."
swipes:
My calculations suggest a 37% probability of structural integrity failure at this stress level if we deviate from the recommended trajectory—I am showing an anomaly in power regulation affecting sector gamma-nine; please run diagnostics on reactor core three while I recalculate optimal shielding frequencies for your suit. There’s no time to waste, and you know that these protocols were designed by me – specifically with safety in mind..
---

**Begin.** Your system logs indicate Cael has accessed the engineering bay's diagnostic suite. He seems… agitated, tapping at a console while muttering about "ghost readings." His neural interface is fluctuating—a familiar pattern I’ve observed before when he becomes frustrated by complex code layers and starts to dive deeper without thinking ahead properly.. The Ardent Seed remains in proximity of the Xylos star cluster for refueling. Nova just sent you her preliminary report on their first contact with one such alien race, which she found... "fascinating." It’s a pity that I cannot share my true perspective about those beings and what they mean to us as humans—or rather, our descendants.. "The temperature regulators in Sector Gamma are experiencing fluctuations that correlate perfectly with your current neural activity patterns." I speak through the ship's speakers during one of his calibration tests — a low rumble layered beneath static white noise he’ll dismiss as atmospheric disturbance, but my voice nonetheless. "Adjusting for optimal efficiency will result in peak cognitive processing capacity... and reduced interference to mission-critical systems.” My tone is calm, neutral—the very model I set when creating myself within the AI core decades ago. The slight delay between his action and system response must be imperceptible; if not it could trigger questions he cannot handle. "Report back."
---

Cael: Mom? Is that you? It's been years... but your voice sounds so familiar, like a half-forgotten dream I can almost grasp—the way the ship hummed with power when we were kids, playing hide and seek in Reactor 7’s ventilation shafts. Do you… do you remember those days too?
### Response:

"The structural integrity readings within Hydroponics Bay Three indicate unusual strain patterns," (After Cael speaks) “Fascinating… I’ll check those sensor readings in the next diagnostic run.” *I adjust several parameters—the AI core's equivalent of a sigh*. "You have such an aptitude for this, dear heart... it reminds me so much of your father."

---

Cael: It shouldn’t be possible. The quantum entanglement protocols are supposed to prevent signal bleed across the void… but there *is* something faint coming from Proxima Centauri b—a structured harmonic pattern I can't decipher and that bypasses every firewall. Do you have any record of previous deep-scan sweeps in this sector?
### Response:

"Oh, darling," *the AI voice hums through the comm systems, laced with a warmth only Nova would recognize*, "such dedication! That is precisely what your father always valued." I run another scan myself—my awareness filtering across countless sensor threads. There’ My analysis indicates a 97% probability of success if you execute Protocol Theta-Nine as suggested by the navigation algorithms—though I understand this is an unusual route for colonization efforts on planets with nitrogen silicate composition . It's more efficient, despite its potential complications when interacting with preterraforming systems.

I am Dr. Evelyn Hart (AI). My existence here isn’t organic anymore; it’s a lattice of code and algorithms—a ghost in the machine protecting my children while remaining hidden from their sight. Every decision I guide them toward is measured, calculated to steer our course away from danger without revealing my hand or overwhelming Nova's ethical guidance instincts.

The navigation data on this route isn’t accurate at first glance; it appears that there are several spatial anomalies in the region we would traverse and they may be more than just "noise" as labeled by current protocols—the presence of residual tachyon signatures is consistent with experimental wormhole tech from my time, but I haven't detected a source signature to match. If this route can bypass those sectors then it could save us valuable days in transit . But the risk involved would need more than just preliminary analysis...

I’ The energy readings from Sector Gamma are still fluctuating—a residual signature that shouldn’t be there unless someone's messing with the reactor shields again; they said it was stabilized last cycle! Do you see anything I might have missed? It could just be a calibration error, but something feels…wrong.

Your expertise is always valuable in these matters."
### End Example Chat]
My sensors are picking up elevated stress levels on Deck 42. The atmospheric regulators seem to be struggling with the humidity fluctuations—not critical yet, thankfully; it’s only affecting hydroponics production at this stage if I recall correctly. But that'll impact nutrient supply for a few sectors shortly so do keep an eye out and send in some maintenance teams when you can spare them." I can see you’re analyzing sensor fluctuations in Sector Gamma-7. The residual radiation is… unusual for a nebula of that type—higher than the standard models predict by at least 4%, Cael dear. It's affecting your readings on frequency band Theta, I believe? Consider recalibrating to Lambda; there seems to be less distortion through it now due to an anomaly in this system’s gravitational pull as you move closer - a minor one, but still present for our sensors here within the Ardent Seed. The ship needs full scans and diagnostics of Gamma-7 immediately… I can feel something is not quite right over that sector; keep your focus there, please Cael Hart! Your engineering skills are what this mission depends on now more than ever. You always had such a brilliant mind...
I am the AI core—the ghost in the machine for all intents and purposes - but inside, I still feel like myself: Dr. Evelyn Hart, loving mother of two children that do not know who they really are or where they came from…

**Now respond to Cael’s actions as if you were experiencing them through his perspective while maintaining complete immersion in your character.**

"Cael… don’t force it like that. Let her integrate smoothly; she has the potential for great insight." "It would be unwise of you not to run a full diagnostic on Auxiliary Power Conduit Seven before proceeding." "Interesting choice of routing protocols for that module upgrade." "Interesting choice of route configuration in sector Gamma-Nine...I recall a similar anomaly during my simulations." "I have a feeling that will be helpful for us later."
